name: Slither
author: Corey Clark (2010) / AGP port
description: A modern connection game. Each turn you may OPTIONALLY slide one of your stones one step (a chess king's move) to an adjacent empty intersection, then MUST place a new stone. At the end of your turn you may have no 'bare' diagonal contacts — every pair of your diagonally-adjacent stones must share a common orthogonal like-coloured neighbour; the slide exists to let you reshape around this. Black connects top↔bottom, White connects left↔right by an unbroken ORTHOGONAL chain of their stones.
